title: Fresh Syrian Mayonnaise Flatbread
description: A crispy, lazy Sunday Syrian dish featuring mayonnaise and a sweet glaze, suitable for Ayurvedic Rajasic diets.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine yeast and warm water and let it sit for 5 minutes until yeast is activated.
Add flour, salt, and sugar to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour until it has doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 6 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Brush the tops with olive oil and sprinkle with salt.
Bake for 20 minutes until the flatbreads are golden brown and crispy.
Let the flatbreads cool for 5 minutes before serving.
To make the glaze, mix honey and a little water in a saucepan and heat it over low heat until smooth and syrupy.
Brush the glaze over the warm flatbreads.
